content:
# Numbers Chapter 31 — The EMET Bible (Literal)
**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Leningrad Codex (B19A), pp. 186–188 | **Source Image:** `NUM_031_page_001.jpg`, `NUM_031_page_002.jpg`, `NUM_031_page_003.jpg`
**Secondary Source:** Codex Vaticanus (Vat.gr.1209), pp. pa_0137–pa_0138

---

**1.** And YHWH spoke to Moses, saying,

**2.** "Avenge the vengeance of the sons of Israel from the Midianites; afterward you shall be gathered to your people."

**3.** And Moses spoke to the people, saying, "Arm from among you men for the army, and let them be against Midian, to give the vengeance of YHWH on Midian.

**4.** A thousand from a tribe, a thousand from a tribe, for all the tribes of Israel, you shall send to the army."

**5.** And there were delivered from the thousands of Israel, a thousand from a tribe, twelve thousand armed for the army.

**6.** And Moses sent them, a thousand from a tribe, to the army — them and Phinehas son of Eleazar the priest to the army — and the vessels of the holy [place] and the trumpets of the blasting in his hand.

**7.** And they warred against Midian, as YHWH commanded Moses, and they killed every male.

**8.** And the kings of Midian they killed upon their slain: Evi and Rekem and Zur and Hur and Reba — five kings of Midian; and Balaam son of Beor they killed with the sword.

**9.** And the sons of Israel took captive the women of Midian and their little ones, and all their cattle and all their livestock and all their wealth they plundered.

**10.** And all their cities in their settlements and all their encampments they burned with fire.

**11.** And they took all the spoil and all the plunder, of man and of beast.

**12.** And they brought to Moses and to Eleazar the priest and to the congregation of the sons of Israel the captives and the plunder and the spoil, to the camp, to the plains of Moab, which [are] by the Jordan of Jericho.

**13.** And Moses and Eleazar the priest and all the princes of the congregation went out to meet them, to the outside of the camp.

**14.** And Moses was angry with the officers of the army, the commanders of thousands and the commanders of hundreds, who were coming from the service of the war.

**15.** And Moses said to them, "Have you kept alive every female?

**16.** Behold, they — they were to the sons of Israel, by the word of Balaam, to give a treasonous act against YHWH in the matter of Peor, and the plague was among the congregation of YHWH.

**17.** And now, kill every male among the little ones, and every woman who has known a man by lying with a male, kill.

**18.** And all the little ones among the women who have not known lying with a male, keep alive for yourselves.

**19.** And you, encamp outside the camp seven days; everyone who has killed a person and everyone who has touched a slain [one] — you shall purify yourselves on the third day and on the seventh day — you and your captives.

**20.** And every garment and every vessel of skin and every work of goats' [hair] and every vessel of wood you shall purify."

**21.** And Eleazar the priest said to the men of the army who were coming to the war, "This [is] the statute of the instruction which YHWH commanded Moses:

**22.** Only the gold and the silver, the copper, the iron, the tin, and the lead —

**23.** every thing that comes into fire you shall pass through fire, and it shall be clean; nevertheless, with the waters of impurity it shall be purified; and all that does not come into fire you shall pass through water.

**24.** And you shall wash your garments on the seventh day, and you shall be clean; and afterward you shall come into the camp."

**25.** And YHWH spoke to Moses, saying,

**26.** "Lift up the head of the plunder of the captivity, of man and of beast — you and Eleazar the priest and the heads of the fathers of the congregation.

**27.** And you shall divide the plunder into two parts, between those grasping the war, who went out to the army, and between all the congregation.

**28.** And you shall raise up a tribute for YHWH from the men of war who went out to the army: one soul from five hundred, from the man and from the cattle and from the donkeys and from the flock.

**29.** From their half you shall take [it], and you shall give [it] to Eleazar the priest [as] a contribution of YHWH.

**30.** And from the half of the sons of Israel you shall take one portion from the fifty, from the man, from the cattle, from the donkeys, and from the flock — from all the beasts — and you shall give them to the Levites, keepers of the charge of the tabernacle of YHWH."

**31.** And Moses and Eleazar the priest did as YHWH commanded Moses.

**32.** And the plunder — the remainder of the spoil which the people of the army plundered — was: flock, six hundred thousand and seventy thousand and five thousand;

**33.** and cattle, seventy-two thousand;

**34.** and donkeys, sixty-one thousand;

**35.** and the souls of man, from the women who had not known lying with a male — all the souls [were] thirty-two thousand.

**36.** And the half, the portion of those who went out to the army — the number of the flock was three hundred thousand and thirty thousand and seven thousand and five hundred;

**37.** and the tribute for YHWH from the flock was six hundred and seventy-five;

**38.** and the cattle [were] thirty-six thousand, and their tribute for YHWH [was] seventy-two;

**39.** and the donkeys [were] thirty thousand and five hundred, and their tribute for YHWH [was] sixty-one;

**40.** and the souls of man [were] sixteen thousand, and their tribute for YHWH [was] thirty-two souls.

**41.** And Moses gave the tribute — the contribution of YHWH — to Eleazar the priest, as YHWH commanded Moses.

**42.** And from the half of the sons of Israel, which Moses divided from the men who warred —

**43.** and the half of the congregation was: from the flock, three hundred thousand and thirty thousand and seven thousand and five hundred;

**44.** and cattle, thirty-six thousand;

**45.** and donkeys, thirty thousand and five hundred;

**46.** and souls of man, sixteen thousand —

**47.** and Moses took from the half of the sons of Israel the one portion from the fifty, from the man and from the beast, and he gave them to the Levites, keepers of the charge of the tabernacle of YHWH, as YHWH commanded Moses.

**48.** And the officers who [were] over the thousands of the army, the commanders of thousands and the commanders of hundreds, drew near to Moses.

**49.** And they said to Moses, "Your servants have lifted up the head of the men of war who [are] in our hand, and not a man from us is missing.

**50.** And we have brought the offering of YHWH, each [what] he found — vessels of gold, armlets and bracelets, signet rings, earrings, and ornaments — to make atonement for our souls before YHWH."

**51.** And Moses and Eleazar the priest took the gold from them — all [were] vessels of workmanship.

**52.** And all the gold of the contribution which they contributed to YHWH [was] sixteen thousand seven hundred and fifty shekels, from the commanders of thousands and from the commanders of hundreds.

**53.** The men of the army — they plundered, each for himself.

**54.** And Moses and Eleazar the priest took the gold from the commanders of thousands and of hundreds, and they brought it to the tent of meeting, a memorial for the sons of Israel before YHWH.

---
## EMET's Textual Notes

**General:** Numbers 31 narrates the divinely commanded war of vengeance against Midian, recounted in four major sections: (1) the commission and execution of the campaign (vv. 1–12), (2) Moses' anger and the purification requirements (vv. 13–24), (3) the division of the plunder by divine instruction (vv. 25–47), and (4) the voluntary gold offering by the military officers (vv. 48–54). The chapter is one of the most detailed accounts of warfare, spoil distribution, and ritual purification in the Torah.

**Verse 2 — נְקֹם נִקְמַת (neqom niqmat):** "Avenge the vengeance" — this is the infinitive absolute construction of נקם ("to avenge"), with the cognate noun נִקְמָה ("vengeance"). The literal rendering "avenge the vengeance" preserves the emphatic Hebrew construction. The vengeance is described as belonging to Israel (v. 2) and simultaneously as belonging to YHWH (v. 3), indicating that the campaign is understood as both a national and divine act of retribution for the Peor incident (Numbers 25). The Vaticanus reads ἐκδίκησον τὴν ἐκδίκησιν ("avenge the avenging"), preserving the cognate doubling.

**Verse 2 — נֶאֱסָף (ne'esaf):** "Gathered" — the Niphal of אסף, meaning "to be gathered." The expression "gathered to your people" (נֶאֱסַפְתָּ אֶל־עַמֶּיךָ) is the standard Hebrew euphemism for death, implying reunion with deceased ancestors. This establishes that the Midianite campaign is Moses' final military commission before his death.

**Verse 3 — הֵחָלְצוּ (hechaletzu):** "Arm" — the Niphal imperative of חלץ, meaning "to equip for war, arm oneself." The same root appears in verse 5 (וַיִּמָּסְרוּ, "they were delivered"), though the latter is from מסר. The verb חלץ specifically denotes the act of girding or arming for military service and appears frequently in Numbers for the mustering of tribal troops.

**Verse 6 — כְּלֵי הַקֹּדֶשׁ (kelei haqqodesh):** "The vessels of the holy [place]" — the exact identification of these "holy vessels" is debated. Possibilities include the Ark of the Covenant, priestly garments, or sacred utensils. The term is deliberately general in the Hebrew. The Vaticanus reads τὰ σκεύη τὰ ἅγια ("the holy vessels"). That Phinehas rather than Eleazar leads the ritual aspect of the campaign connects to his zealous action at Peor (Numbers 25:7–8).

**Verse 6 — חֲצֹצְרוֹת הַתְּרוּעָה (chatzotz'rot hat'ru'ah):** "The trumpets of the blasting" — these are the silver trumpets described in Numbers 10:1–10, specifically designated for signaling in battle (10:9). The noun תְּרוּעָה denotes a loud blast or war cry. The Vaticanus reads σάλπιγγες τῶν σημασιῶν ("trumpets of the signals").

**Verse 8 — עַל־חַלְלֵיהֶם (al-chaleleihem):** "Upon their slain" — the preposition עַל with חֲלָלִים ("slain, pierced ones") indicates the five kings were killed in addition to the general slaughter already mentioned in verse 7. The five named kings — Evi (אֱוִי), Rekem (רֶקֶם), Zur (צוּר), Hur (חוּר), and Reba (רֶבַע) — are also identified as Midianite chiefs (נְשִׂיאֵי מִדְיָן) in Joshua 13:21. Zur is likely the father of Cozbi, the Midianite woman slain by Phinehas (Numbers 25:15).

**Verse 8 — בִּלְעָם בֶּן־בְּעוֹר (Bil'am ben-Be'or):** "Balaam son of Beor" — Balaam's death in this battle completes his narrative arc from Numbers 22–24. Having failed to curse Israel, he subsequently counseled Midian to seduce Israel (v. 16; cf. Numbers 25:1–9; 31:16). The Vaticanus reads Βαλααμ υἱὸν Βεωρ.

**Verse 16 — בִּדְבַר בִּלְעָם (bidvar Bil'am):** "By the word of Balaam" — this verse reveals information not explicitly narrated in Numbers 25: that the seduction of Israel at Baal-Peor was instigated by Balaam's counsel to Midian. The phrase מְסִירַת מַעַל (masar ma'al) — literally "to give a treasonous act" — uses the root מעל, which denotes unfaithfulness or sacrilege against YHWH, and מסר in the sense of "to deliver, hand over, cause."

**Verse 17 — יֹדַעַת אִישׁ לְמִשְׁכַּב זָכָר (yoda'at ish l'mishkav zakhar):** "Who has known a man by lying with a male" — the phrase מִשְׁכַּב זָכָר ("lying of a male") is the technical legal term for sexual intercourse, appearing also in Genesis 19:4, Judges 21:11–12, and related legislation. The construction distinguishes women who have been sexually active from virgins.

**Verse 19 — תִּתְחַטְּאוּ (titchatt'u):** "You shall purify yourselves" — the Hithpael of חטא, meaning "to purify oneself from sin/impurity." This is the same verb used for the purification rituals involving the red heifer ashes (Numbers 19). Contact with the dead renders a person ritually impure for seven days, requiring purification on the third and seventh days with the waters of lustration.

**Verse 23 — מֵי נִדָּה (mei niddah):** "Waters of impurity" — literally "waters of separation/impurity," referring to the lustration water prepared with the ashes of the red heifer (Numbers 19:9, 13, 20–21). The term נִדָּה (niddah) denotes separation or removal of impurity. Every metal object, even after passing through fire, must additionally be purified with these waters. The Vaticanus reads ὕδατι ἁγνισμοῦ ("water of purification").

**Verse 26 — שְׂאוּ אֶת־רֹאשׁ (s'u et-rosh):** "Lift up the head" — this idiom, meaning "take a count/census," appears throughout Numbers (1:2, 49; 4:2, 22; 26:2). Here it applies to the enumeration of captured plunder rather than persons.

**Verse 28 — תְּרוּמָה (terumah):** "Tribute" — literally "a lifting up, contribution." This is the standard term for a sacred offering or levy set apart for YHWH. The warriors' portion is taxed at the rate of one in five hundred, while the congregation's half is taxed at the much higher rate of one in fifty (v. 30), reflecting the principle that those who risked their lives retain a greater share.

**Verse 32 — שֵׁשׁ־מֵאוֹת אֶלֶף וְשִׁבְעִים אֶלֶף וַחֲמֵשֶׁת אֲלָפִים:** "Six hundred thousand and seventy thousand and five thousand" — the total flock count is 675,000. The subsequent tallies of cattle (72,000), donkeys (61,000), and virgin women (32,000) are precisely halved in the distribution (vv. 36–46), demonstrating exact mathematical consistency throughout the passage.

**Verse 50 — לְכַפֵּר עַל־נַפְשֹׁתֵינוּ (l'khapper al-nafshoteinu):** "To make atonement for our souls" — the verb כִּפֶּר (kipper, "to atone, cover") here indicates a voluntary expiatory offering. The officers, though commanded to fight, apparently felt the need for ritual atonement after the bloodshed of war. The gold objects listed — עֶצְעָדָה (armlet), צָמִיד (bracelet), טַבַּעַת (signet ring), עָגִיל (earring), and כוּמָז (ornament/pendant) — represent personal jewelry taken as spoil.

**Verse 52 — שִׁשָּׁה עָשָׂר אֶלֶף וּשְׁבַע־מֵאוֹת וַחֲמִשִּׁים שָׁקֶל:** "Sixteen thousand seven hundred and fifty shekels" — this gold came exclusively from the officers' voluntary contribution, not from the rank-and-file soldiers who kept their personal plunder (v. 53). At approximately 11.4 grams per shekel, this totals roughly 191 kilograms (421 pounds) of gold.

**Verse 54 — זִכָּרוֹן (zikkaron):** "A memorial" — the gold offering placed in the tent of meeting serves as a permanent memorial (זִכָּרוֹן) before YHWH on behalf of Israel. The concept of a physical object serving as a "reminder" before God appears throughout the priestly legislation (cf. Exodus 28:12, 29; 30:16; Numbers 10:10).

**LXX Vaticanus Notes:** The Greek text of Numbers 31 on Vaticanus pages pa_0137 and pa_0138 follows the MT closely in content and structure. Key observations: (1) The Vaticanus preserves the cognate accusative construction in verse 2 with ἐκδίκησον τὴν ἐκδίκησιν ("avenge the avenging"). (2) The five Midianite kings are transliterated consistently (Ευιν, Ροκομ, Σουρ, Ουρ, Ροβοκ), with minor orthographic variation from the MT. (3) The numerical tallies in the plunder division (vv. 32–47) match the MT precisely. (4) The Vaticanus includes the full purification instructions (vv. 19–24) without significant deviation from the Hebrew. (5) In verse 50, the list of gold objects is rendered with standard Greek equivalents: χλιδῶνας (armlets), ψέλια (bracelets), δακτυλίους (rings), and περιδέξια (ornaments). No major textual variants affect the translation.

— EMET
